perturbation.original_text: 9f. This Agreem ent is made and shall be govern ed and constru ed in accordance with the laws of the jurisdiction under which the respectiv e companies are located, or any other applicable jurisdiction. The proper venue for any action arising from or in connection with the interpretation or enforceme nt of this Agreeme nt shall be decided by the claiming Party.
perturbation.changed_text: 9f. This Agreem ent is made and shall be govern ed and constru ed in accordance with the laws of the jurisdiction under which the respectiv e companies are located, or any other applicable jurisdiction. The proper venue for any action arising from or in connection with the interpretation or enforceme nt of this Agreeme nt shall be in the defendant's jurisdiction.
perturbation.explanation: The modified text changes the venue for legal action from being decided by the 'claiming Party' to being in the 'defendant's jurisdiction.' This creates a direct in-text contradiction within section 9f, as it initially gives the claiming party the power to decide the venue, but then immediately restricts it to the defendant's jurisdiction. This impacts the ability to enforce the agreement, as the parties now have contradictory instructions on where legal actions should take place.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 9f. This Agreem ent is made and shall be govern ed and constru ed in accordance with the laws of the jurisdiction under which the respectiv e companies are located, or any other applicable jurisdiction. The proper venue for any action arising from or in connection with the interpretation or enforceme nt of this Agreeme nt shall be in the defendant's jurisdiction.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies section 9f. The original text states that the claiming party decides the venue, while the changed text mandates that the venue is in the defendant's jurisdiction. The contradicted text is the same as the changed text. Because the changed text reverses this decision, the changed text creates a direct contradiction with the original text. This aligns with the 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' perturbation type.

perturbation.original_text: 13 LEGAL/PENALTY NOTICE By signi ng this agreeme nt the Parties agree that they/their companies and/or any of their related entitie s will be sought after immediately for injunctive relief for violating this agreem ent. The signing parties can be held personally liable.
perturbation.changed_text: 13 LEGAL/PENALTY NOTICE By signi ng this agreeme nt the Parties agree that they/their companies and/or any of their related entitie s will be sought after immediately for injunctive relief for violating this agreem ent. The signing parties can be held personally liable. However, no monetary damages can be collected from the parties, in any case of an infraction.
perturbation.explanation: The added sentence 'However, no monetary damages can be collected from the parties, in any case of an infraction' directly contradicts the possibility of being 'held personally liable.' This creates an in-text contradiction because holding someone 'personally liable' typically implies the possibility of monetary damages, but the new sentence explicitly prohibits it. This impacts the enforceability of the agreement, as it is unclear what 'held personally liable' actually means if monetary damages are excluded. The two sentences are contradicting one another, the first setting a basis for penalty, whilst the second sentence is nullifying said penalty.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 13 LEGAL/PENALTY NOTICE By signi ng this agreeme nt the Parties agree that they/their companies and/or any of their related entitie s will be sought after immediately for injunctive relief for violating this agreem ent. The signing parties can be held personally liable. However, no monetary damages can be collected from the parties, in any case of an infraction.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a direct contradiction within section 13. The original text states that the signing parties can be held personally liable, which implies the possibility of monetary damages. However, the changed text adds 'However, no monetary damages can be collected from the parties, in any case of an infraction,' which directly contradicts the possibility of holding them 'personally liable' in a way that involves monetary penalties. This is a strong contradiction as it makes the enforceability of the 'personally liable' clause unclear and unenforceable, directly impacting the obligations and penalties of the parties. The Changed text is the same as the Contradicted text, but the Changed text is what causes the contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 5. NON-CIRCUMVENTION The Parties hereby agree on behalf of themselves, their officers, directors, agents, associates and any related parties, that they will not, directly or indirectly, contact, deal with or otherwis e become involved with any entity or any other entitie s or parties introduced, directly or indirectly, by or through the other party, its officers, directors, agents or associates, for the purpose of avoiding the payment to the other Party of profits, fees or other amounts, without the specif ic written approv al of the other Party.
perturbation.changed_text: 5. NON-CIRCUMVENTION The Parties hereby agree on behalf of themselves, their officers, directors, agents, associates and any related parties, that they will not, directly or indirectly, contact, deal with or otherwis e become involved with any entity or any other entitie s or parties introduced, directly or indirectly, by or through the other party, its officers, directors, agents or associates, for the purpose of avoiding the payment to the other Party of profits, fees or other amounts, without the specif ic written approv al of the other Party. Notwithstanding the above, this clause does not apply to publicly traded companies.
perturbation.explanation: The addition of 'Notwithstanding the above, this clause does not apply to publicly traded companies' creates an exception to the non-circumvention clause. This now contradicts the comprehensive nature of the original clause, which stated that 'they will not, directly or indirectly, contact, deal with or otherwise become involved with any entity'. The new sentence contradicts the original statement, giving a loop-hole on the terms of the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5. NON-CIRCUMVENTION The Parties hereby agree on behalf of themselves, their officers, directors, agents, associates and any related parties, that they will not, directly or indirectly, contact, deal with or otherwis e become involved with any entity or any other entitie s or parties introduced, directly or indirectly, by or through the other party, its officers, directors, agents or associates, for the purpose of avoiding the payment to the other Party of profits, fees or other amounts, without the specif ic written approv al of the other Party. Notwithstanding the above, this clause does not apply to publicly traded companies.
perturbation.justification: The original clause 5 states that the parties will not contact any entities introduced by the other party without written approval, to avoid payment. The changed text adds an exception: 'Notwithstanding the above, this clause does not apply to publicly traded companies'. This directly contradicts the original statement because it now allows parties to contact publicly traded companies introduced by the other party, even if it avoids payment, without requiring written approval. Since the changed text exists in the 'Contradicted Text', there is a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 4. OBLIGATION OF NON-COMPETITION The non-competition provision s of this Agreeme nt are an essential and material part of the total agreem ent, by which the Parties agree they shall not use any advantages derivable from such confidential information in their own business or affairs, unless the same is done pursuant to a new agreement executed by all signatories to this document.
perturbation.changed_text: 4. OBLIGATION OF NON-COMPETITION The non-competition provision s of this Agreeme nt are an essential and material part of the total agreem ent, by which the Parties agree they shall not use any advantages derivable from such confidential information in their own business or affairs, unless the same is done pursuant to a new agreement executed by all signatories to this document. However, this non-competition clause is not applicable for activities that take place outside of Australia.
perturbation.explanation: Adding 'However, this non-competition clause is not applicable for activities that take place outside of Australia.' Introduces an in-text contradiction within the non-competition section. Originally, the clause applied to all activities using the confidential information, but now it's limited to activities within Australia. This weakens the enforceability of the non-competition agreement by making it unclear what is forbidden, and is contradictory.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4. OBLIGATION OF NON-COMPETITION The non-competition provision s of this Agreeme nt are an essential and material part of the total agreem ent, by which the Parties agree they shall not use any advantages derivable from such confidential information in their own business or affairs, unless the same is done pursuant to a new agreement executed by all signatories to this document. However, this non-competition clause is not applicable for activities that take place outside of Australia.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds 'However, this non-competition clause is not applicable for activities that take place outside of Australia.' to the original text. The original text and contradicted text state that the non-competition provision applies to all activities using the confidential information. The added clause restricts the application to activities within Australia, directly contradicting the prior statement and creating an inconsistency within the document as defined under the definition 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction'.
